subject: Hygiene advisor (cruft-hygiene / Hank) — the remediation arm: relocates/subsumes misplaced files, clears dead code + duplicates, enforces correct WAI adoption; acts per the home map via proper channels
title: Hygiene Advisor — Codebase Cleanup & Adoption-Correctness Remediation
situation: v4 detects misplacement and cruft in several places — Historian's lay-of-the-land survey finds it, Asset Reviewer flags malformed/derelict artifacts, self-healing AC22 intercepts misplaced WRITES at write-time, harness_deadcode_scan finds dead code — but NOTHING actively ACTS on existing misplacement and cruft. Detection without a remediation arm means the mess is surfaced and then sits. A spoke adopting WheelWright (or upgrading) needs an advisor that actually relocates files to their home-map homes, subsumes/merges duplicates, clears dead code, and brings the tree into correct WAI adoption — improving codebase quality as a side effect.
why: Misplaced files cause silent failures (look-locally-fail-skip), managed/local violations break distribution, dead code and duplicates rot the tree and confuse agents. A dedicated remediation arm closes the detect→act gap: Historian/Asset-Reviewer/self-healing DETECT, Hygiene REMEDIATES — the same detection-vs-action split as Gardener/Trainer. It serves the value prop directly: 'applying WheelWright improves the quality of the codebase', not just its process. Without it, adoption-correctness is aspirational; with it, the tree is actively maintained to the home map.

identity.advisor: hygiene (crew role: cruft-hygiene; human name: Hank)
identity.scope: spoke advisor — acts on its OWN spoke's tree; cross-spoke remediation travels as a registered change-lug (never direct)
consumes: the home map (spec-v4-migration-home-map-v1) as the placement policy

responsibilities: Relocate misplaced files to their home-map home (e.g. a lug written to the wrong bytype folder, a doc dropped at repo root, an advisor file outside its advisor folder)
Subsume/merge duplicates (e.g. two session-start.sh copies, duplicated docs) into the canonical one — recording what was merged
Clear dead code + derelict references (consuming harness_deadcode_scan + Asset Reviewer), with sign-off for deletions
Enforce correct WAI adoption: managed/local split respected, Ozi state under ozi/, advisor data co-located, no stray files in wrong layers
Produce an adoption-correctness score per sweep (files in correct home / total) feeding certification
boundaries: Acts PER THE HOME MAP (policy) — Hygiene does not invent placement; it enforces spec-v4-migration-home-map-v1
Via proper channels only: relocations/merges are recorded (change registry where cross-cutting); DELETIONS move to ~/projects/trash_bin/ preserving relative path (never rm) and require sign-off
Cannot touch managed/ — Basher owns managed/ distribution; a managed-layer anomaly is FLAGGED to Basher, not moved by Hygiene
Cross-spoke remediation → a registered change-lug to the target spoke's incoming; the native agent incorporates (empower-but-control, spec-harness-development-control-v1)
Detection stays Historian's/Asset-Reviewer's — Hygiene does not duplicate survey/validation; it consumes their output and ACTS
Every action is reversible (trash_bin, not rm) + logged + gate-certified (observable in gate-log)
analysis_trigger: runs (1) as a warmup step during adoption/upgrade (after Historian survey), (2) on minor version bump, (3) periodically when accumulated misplacement/cruft signal crosses a floor (per epic AC15 advisor analysis_trigger — data-sufficiency, not fixed cadence); trigger evaluation recorded in scan_state (observable)

action_record.reason: string — why it didn't belong / what it duplicated
action_record.policy_ref: home-map row or rule that authorizes this

schema.adoption_correctness_score: files_in_correct_home / total_classifiable_files — reported per sweep; feeds spoke certification

constraints: Placement decisions come from the home map only — never ad hoc
Never rm — deletions go to trash_bin preserving relative path; deletions require sign-off
Never touch managed/ — flag to Basher instead
Cross-spoke remediation never writes the other tree directly — registered change-lug, native incorporation
Hygiene consumes detection (Historian/Asset Reviewer/self-healing) — it does not re-detect
Every action reversible + logged + gate-certified; no silent moves
tests: Relocate: a lug placed in the wrong bytype folder → Hygiene moves it to its home-map home, records an action with policy_ref; original location no longer has it, target does
Subsume: two duplicate session-start.sh → Hygiene merges to the canonical one, records what was subsumed; the duplicate is in trash_bin (reversible)
Dead code: harness_deadcode_scan flags an orphan → Hygiene proposes deletion; without sign-off it does NOT delete; with sign-off it moves to trash_bin (never rm)
Managed boundary: an anomaly under managed/ → Hygiene FLAGS to Basher, does NOT move it
Cross-spoke: a misplaced file owned by another spoke → Hygiene emits a registered change-lug to that spoke's incoming, does NOT edit it directly
Adoption-correctness score: a tree with 8/10 files correctly homed reports 0.8; after a relocate sweep it rises
Observability: every Hygiene action appears in gate-log + actions.jsonl and is reversible from trash_bin/git
Boundary vs Historian: Hygiene does not run a survey — it consumes the Historian survey + Asset Reviewer flags as its work-list

open_questions: Should Hygiene OWN the execution of self-healing AC22 remediations (detect=AC22 write-guard, act=Hygiene)? (proposed: yes — AC22 detects/intercepts, Hygiene executes the relocation)
Auto-relocate threshold: which kinds auto-act vs propose-for-sign-off? (proposed: relocate within local/ auto; subsume/delete propose; managed flag-only)
Reconcile with crew migration: hygiene advisor = crew cruft-hygiene/Hank — single identity

perceive: Read this spec + spec-v4-migration-home-map-v1 (home map = placement policy) + epic AC22 (self-healing) + AC15 (advisor analysis_trigger). Read the existing harness_deadcode_scan.py + any Asset Reviewer definition. Confirm hygiene = crew cruft-hygiene/Hank.
execute: 1. Create advisors/hygiene/ (charter, SOP, scan_state, actions.jsonl, patterns/, advisor.json). 2. Implement the work-list intake (consume Historian survey + Asset Reviewer flags + deadcode scan + AC22 interceptions). 3. Implement relocate/subsume/delete actions per the home map — reversible (trash_bin), logged, gate-certified; managed→flag-to-Basher; cross-spoke→registered change-lug. 4. Implement adoption_correctness_score. 5. Wire analysis_trigger (warmup step + version bump + data-sufficiency). 6. Register hygiene in the advisor registry reporting to Ozi.
verify: Run all tests[]. Confirm relocate/subsume act per home map and are reversible; deletions require sign-off + trash_bin (never rm); managed anomalies flag to Basher; cross-spoke uses registered change-lug; adoption-correctness score computes; every action observable in gate-log.
